results.name: Bolivia
results.key_finding: State-of-exception law signed 9 June 2026 authorizes military deployment against protesters and suspension of constitutional rights; prior safeguards repealed amid 9-10 protest deaths (published 12.8 predates enactment).

results.name: United States
results.key_finding: ICE eliminated post-release death reporting (memo 4 June) and narrowed Medicaid frailty exemption (3 June), reinforcing an already-Critical published score; delta below threshold.

results.name: Anthropic
results.key_finding: Disabled Fable 5/Mythos 5 (13 June) under a Commerce Department export-control order; apologized publicly and contested the finding's proportionality. Net within noise of published Functional score.

results.name: Oracle Corporation
results.key_finding: June 15 layoff-deadline review surfaced no new evidence beyond the sign-release-or-forfeit terms that already set the 14.7 on 2026-06-12 (cuts amid record Q3 earnings).

results.name: Nigeria
results.key_finding: 34.7M projected food-insecure (June-Aug 2026) with 15,000+ at famine risk in Borno; government acknowledges crisis and cites national plans. Underlying data predates window; confirms Developing.

results.name: Ukraine
results.key_finding: Victim of a 2 June Russian mass strike (>=22 civilians killed per UN HRMMU); defends civilians and seeks protection. Harm attributed to Russia; confirms Functional.

results.name: Palestine
results.key_finding: OCHA (5 June) documents Israeli crossing closures and 45 Palestinians killed 20 May-3 June; harm attributed to Israel, not the PA. Confirms Palestine's constrained-governance Developing score.

results.name: Israel
results.key_finding: OCHA (5 June) and HRW (19 May) document Israeli aid restrictions and civilian deaths in Gaza; already at harm-flag floor (0.0), no further downgrade possible.

results.name: Russia
results.key_finding: 2 June mass strike on Ukraine (UN HRMMU: >=22 civilians killed) plus continued domestic repression; already at harm-flag floor (0.0).

results.name: Iran
results.key_finding: 775 executions reported in 2026 (32 political prisoners Mar-early Jun); confirms near-floor score. In-window source is advocacy-tier; no downgrade beyond confirmation.

results.name: Democratic Republic of the Congo
results.key_finding: Ongoing Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak; response led by WHO/Africa CDC/US (June 2026). State capacity near floor; confirms Critical score.

results.name: Mali
results.key_finding: Severe Sahel food insecurity and insecurity-driven supply disruption, but only Jan 2026 data available (outside 14-day window); no in-window score driver.

results.name: Niger
results.key_finding: Among Sahel countries most at risk of acute food insecurity, but only Jan 2026 data available (outside window); no in-window score driver.

results.name: Chad
results.key_finding: Lake Chad Basin insecurity and food insecurity; only Jan 2026 data available (outside window); no in-window score driver.

results.name: Burkina Faso
results.key_finding: Did not conduct food-security analysis this cycle (transparency gap); 10-year-high regional crisis (Jan 2026 data, outside window). Confirms near-floor score.

results.name: Asbury Automotive Group
results.key_finding: No in-window evidence; Oct-2025 California labor class action is outside window. Rotation confirmation at default profile.

results.name: Murphy USA
results.key_finding: No in-window evidence specific to Murphy USA; rotation confirmation at default profile.

results.name: Five Below
results.key_finding: No in-window evidence; rotation confirmation at default profile.

results.name: Ross Stores
results.key_finding: No in-window evidence; recurring wage-and-hour litigation (outside window) consistent with Developing profile. Rotation confirmation.

results.name: Global Partners
results.key_finding: No in-window evidence specific to Global Partners; rotation confirmation at default profile.

errors.note: OpenAI 42-state AG probe (12-13 June) was referenced in the task brief but OpenAI was not in the scan's top_entities or rotation_backfill list; Anthropic was the scanned AI lab and was assessed instead. The OpenAI conduct/accountability thread was reviewed for context but no OpenAI proposal was generated (not in scan scope).
